body_txt: Impressions have been a staple of comedy for quite a long time, and a staple of bad comedy for about five seconds shorter. Ergo, when one of your characters is a comedian, impressions tend to crop up. If your character is a bad comedian, though, his impression is likely to be sub-par at best. Want to point this out to the audience? Couldn't be easier. Simply have him do one or more of the following:  If a comedian does this intentionally, it's not this trope. Neither is it if he lampshades the fact that his impressions are terrible and otherwise doesn't make them a big part of his routine. See Stylistic Suck , and subtropes such as Bad "Bad Acting" and Hollywood Tone-Deaf . This trope is not about bad Impressionist painters .
